title: Per-project team configuration
description: Project-level config read by the /team interpreter from `.claude/team.config.json`. Makes role→agent, model, and scope resolution deterministic and per-project instead of inferred at runtime. All fields optional — absent values fall back to built-in defaults (see workflows/team.config.example.json for the defaults).

roles.description: Maps a workflow role (used in profile stages) to a concrete agent name passed verbatim as Task `subagent_type`. The agent may be ANY registered agent: a project agent in .claude/agents/<name> (bare name), a user agent in ~/.claude/agents/<name> (bare name), this plugin's agent (fullstack-team:<name>), or another plugin's agent (<plugin>:<name>). Unmapped roles fall back to the built-in default agent of the same name. New role keys (beyond the built-in set) are allowed — reference them from a custom profile or via roster_overrides.

roster_overrides.description: Per-stage roster adjustments applied by the interpreter AFTER a profile's `conditional[]` rules — lets a project customize who runs a stage without forking workflows/*.json. Keyed by stage id (e.g. 'review', 'architecture', 'exploration'). Values are agent names (same resolution as `roles`). `replace` wins over add/remove when present.

add.description: Extra agents to add to this stage's roster.

remove.description: Agents/roles to drop from this stage's roster.

replace.description: Replace the entire roster with exactly these agents.

models.description: Maps a role to a model override (opus|sonnet|haiku). The wildcard key '*' sets the default. Priority: explicit role > '*' > agent's own frontmatter.

scope_map.description: Ordered glob→scope rules. First matching glob (against a touched/planned file) sets the scope and its dev agent. Generated/extended by the /team-scope command (P3).

flags.description: Glob expressions defining scope flags used by `conditional` rules in profiles.

design_system.description: Design system name; the `ui` review role reads ~/.claude/design-systems/<name>.md.
